questions.text: The rate of change of a function y = f(x) with respect to x is given by:
questions.explanation: The derivative df/dx represents the rate of change of y with respect to x.

questions.text: A function has a maximum at x = a if f'(a) = 0 and f''(a) < 0.
questions.explanation: For a maximum: f'(a) = 0 (critical point) and f''(a) < 0 (concave down).

questions.text: The slope of the tangent to a curve at a point is given by ________ at that point.
questions.explanation: The derivative dy/dx at a point gives the slope of the tangent to the curve at that point.

questions.correctAnswer: If f is continuous on [a,b], differentiable on (a,b), and f(a) = f(b), then there exists c in (a,b) such that f'(c) = 0
questions.explanation: Rolle's theorem is a special case of Mean Value Theorem where f(a) = f(b).
